agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to consider joining accessibility research project
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a delegation from Dr. Kishore Seetharaman of Simon Fraser University's Department of Gerontology, inviting the City of Burnaby to participate in a research project on bridging accessibility. This is a proposed partnership, not yet approved. If accepted, the city would take part in a study aimed at improving accessibility for people with disabilities, which could affect local businesses that serve the public.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
5.1 Delegation: Invitation to Participate in Bridging Accessibility Research Project
Purpose: Dr. Kishore Seetharaman, SFU Department of Gerontology
agenda_briefs.article_title: Access Advisory Committee Meeting Agenda — June 17, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Accessibility measures for Burnaby's 2026 local election
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a verbal report from the Burnaby Election Team on proposed accessibility measures for the 2026 General Local Election. This is an early look at what changes might be made to make voting easier for people with disabilities, seniors, and others who face barriers. No decisions have been made yet — this is a discussion to gather input and plan ahead.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1 VERBAL REPORT:  Burnaby Election Team - Accessibility                     Measures for the 2026 General Local Election
Purpose: To provide information regarding accessibility measures for the 2026 general election.
agenda_briefs.article_title: Access Advisory Committee Meeting Agenda — June 17, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to consider access services for children and youth in recreation
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to receive a verbal report on proposed Access Services for children and youth in Burnaby Recreation. This is an early-stage proposal — Council will hear information about the program but has not yet voted on or approved any changes. The report outlines potential ways to make recreation programs more accessible for children and youth, but no new rules or fees have been decided. Business owners who offer recreation services or programs for children and youth should watch for future council decisions that could affect how these services are delivered or funded.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Monitor upcoming council meetings for a formal vote on this proposal — no action is needed now.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2 VERBAL REPORT: Access Services for Children and Youth in Recreation
Purpose: To provide information on Access Services for children and youth in Burnaby Recreation.
agenda_briefs.article_title: Access Advisory Committee Meeting Agenda — June 17, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on 2025/2026 Accessibility Plan updates
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving amendments to the City's Accessibility Plan and hear an update on progress made so far in 2025/2026. This includes completed accessibility improvements to city facilities, services, and information. The plan is meant to remove barriers for people with disabilities. A vote on the updated plan is expected at the upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.industries: Retail, Professional Services, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.3 Accessibility Plan – Implementation Update 2025/2026
Purpose: To provide an update on Accessibility Plan amendments, implementation progress, and completed accessibility improvements in 2025/2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider update on Burnaby Accessibility Feedback Form submissions
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will receive a verbal report on submissions made through the city's Accessibility Feedback Form during April and May 2026. This is an update for the Committee — no new rules or decisions are being voted on yet. The report is meant to keep the public and business owners informed about accessibility issues being raised in the community.
agenda_briefs.industries: Retail, Professional Services, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4 VERBAL REPORT: Accessibility Feedback Form Reporting - April and May 2026
Purpose: To provide the Committee with an update on submissions received through the Accessibility Feedback Form in April and May 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rosser Avenue and Halifax Street intersection changes in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a request from resident Penny Leclair to add a pedestrian signal for crossing Rosser Avenue at the intersection of Rosser Avenue and Halifax Street. The request is driven by safety concerns for a deafblind senior who cannot currently cross Rosser Avenue at this intersection. The existing pedestrian signal only covers Halifax Street. The item was referred from a previous committee meeting and is up for discussion and a potential vote at an upcoming council meeting. Business owners who use this intersection should be aware that traffic patterns or access could change if the proposal is approved.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Monitor the council meeting agenda for the date of the vote on this intersection change request.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1 Penny Leclair - Request for Rosser Avenue and Halifax Street Intersection Changes
(Referred from May 26, 2026 CCP)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to address hospital access concerns
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a proposal from Councillor O. Brassard regarding access concerns at Burnaby Hospital. The proposal, listed as item 9.1 on the upcoming council agenda, has not yet been debated or voted on. If approved, it could lead to changes in how people get to and from the hospital, potentially affecting traffic, parking, and transit routes near the facility. Business owners in the area should watch for the council decision, as it may impact customer access and local traffic patterns.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and consider attending or submitting comments if the proposal affects your business access or parking.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 9.1 O. Brassard: Burnaby Hospital Access Concerns
agenda_briefs.article_title: Access Advisory Committee Meeting Agenda — June 17, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Floating bus stop bike lane at Brentwood Town Centre
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to build a 'floating bus stop' bike lane at Brentwood Town Centre. This design would place the bike lane between the sidewalk and the bus stop, so buses pull into a separate lane to pick up passengers. The change is meant to make cycling safer and reduce conflicts between bikes and buses. The proposal is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect traffic and access near your business at Brentwood.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 9.2 K. Damani: Floating Bus Stop Bike Lane at Brentwood Town Centre
agenda_briefs.article_title: Access Advisory Committee Meeting Agenda — June 17, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Walk with Burnaby Mayor during AccessAbility Week
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed event: a walk with the Mayor during AccessAbility Week, scheduled for May 31 to June 6. This is a community engagement activity, not a new bylaw or regulation. The proposal is on the council agenda for discussion and a vote. If approved, it would promote accessibility and inclusion in Burnaby.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Metrotown pedestrian scramble crossing at Kingsway and Willingdon
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed pedestrian scramble at the busy Metrotown intersection of Kingsway and Willingdon Avenue. A pedestrian scramble stops all vehicle traffic at the same time, allowing people to cross the street in any direction, including diagonally. If approved, this change would affect traffic flow, delivery access, and customer foot traffic for nearby businesses. The proposal is part of the city's Metrotown transportation plan and is up for a council decision at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — if approved, plan for potential traffic disruptions and changes to customer access near the intersection.
